principles.rule_1: Backend Authority: Server is sole source of truth
principles.rule_2: Invariants: Must be enforced server-side, never bypass-able
principles.rule_3: Invariant Failures: Must be explicit errors, not graceful degradation
principles.rule_4: Identity Boundary: Only userId accepted at API boundary

checks.name: userId Scoping
checks.description: All data queries must be scoped by userId, not frontend-provided athleteId
checks.violation: Frontend provides athleteId, server doesn't verify ownership

checks.name: Ownership Verification
checks.description: Before mutating data, verify the user owns it
checks.violation: Mutation happens without verifying user owns the data

checks.name: Repository Scoping
checks.description: Repository constructor accepts userId and enforces scoping
checks.violation: Repository not scoped by userId in constructor

checks.name: Invariant Enforcement
checks.description: Invariants checked and enforced at database level
checks.violation: Invariant checked only on client, not enforced server-side

checks.name: Authentication Boundary
checks.description: Auth check present on all protected routes

checks.name: Error Response Semantics
checks.violation: Wrong status code for error condition

checks.name: Contract Documentation
checks.description: Code marked with CONTRACT: DATA_OWNERSHIP and AUTH_IDENTITY
checks.violation: Ownership enforcement present but not documented with CONTRACT marker

401.usage: User not logged in OR no session

403.meaning: Valid user but no access to resource
403.usage: User authenticated but doesn't own the data

404.usage: Data doesn't exist (can also hide 403 for privacy)

frontend_athleteId.pattern: Frontend passes athleteId directly to API
frontend_athleteId.risk: Any user can fetch any athlete's data
frontend_athleteId.fix: Use userId from session; derive athleteId server-side

no_ownership_check.risk: Any user can modify any data

client_invariant_check.pattern: Client checks invariant, server doesn't enforce
client_invariant_check.risk: Race condition; invariant can be violated
client_invariant_check.fix: Check invariant server-side atomically before mutation

implicit_scoping.pattern: Repository not scoped by userId; each method checks ownership
implicit_scoping.risk: Easy to forget check; hard to audit; repetitive
implicit_scoping.fix: Constructor takes userId; all methods implicitly scoped

graceful_degradation.pattern: Invariant failure returns empty result instead of error
graceful_degradation.risk: Hides real problems; client can't recover properly
graceful_degradation.fix: Return explicit error (409 or 500) for invariant violations
